WeeklyStockGainer.title: WeeklyStockGainer
WeeklyStockGainer.description: Represents a stock that has gained more than 2% compared to its closing price on the same weekday in the previous week, intended for display on the NSE Weekly Gainers dashboard.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WeeklyStockGainer entity.

symbol.description: The unique ticker symbol of the stock, e.g., 'TCS'.

companyName.description: The full registered name of the company associated with the stock symbol.

currentClosePrice.description: The closing price of the stock on the current comparison date.

previousWeekClosePrice.description: The closing price of the stock on the same weekday in the previous week.

percentageChange.description: The percentage change in price, calculated as (Current Close - Previous Week Close) / Previous Week Close * 100. This value is expected to be greater than 2.

volume.description: The total trading volume of the stock on the current comparison date.

comparisonDate.description: The date of the current closing price and comparison, formatted as YYYY-MM-DD.

definition.description: Publicly accessible collection of weekly stock gainers. Each document represents a stock that met the gain criteria. Data is populated by a secure backend process. This collection is universally readable by all application users. It includes no denormalized authorization fields as all data is public.

params.name: stockGainerId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific WeeklyStockGainer document.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for the 'NSE Weekly Gainers' application is a single, top-level collection: `/weekly_stock_gainers`. Each document within this collection will represent a `WeeklyStockGainer` entity. This approach adheres strictly to the core design principles and mandates.

**Authorization Independence:** This structure inherently achieves authorization independence because there are no hierarchical dependencies for authorization. All documents in the `/weekly_stock_gainers` collection are intended to be publicly readable by all users. There are no subcollections whose access rights depend on attributes of a parent document, thus eliminating the need for complex `get()` calls in security rules. Data is managed and populated by a backend process, not by end-users, simplifying write rules to `false` for client-side operations.

**Clarity of Intent & Structural Segregation:** The intent is clear: all documents in this collection represent public, read-only weekly stock gainer data. This collection has a homogeneous security posture (public read-only for all clients). There's no mixing of public and private data within this collection, removing complexity and making security rules straightforward.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** This structure fully supports Queryable and Accessible Pages (QAPs). Since all `WeeklyStockGainer` documents are publicly accessible and share the same security requirements, client-side queries can list and filter documents from the `/weekly_stock_gainers` collection without any security rule constraints beyond basic read access. The application's filtering, sorting, and pagination logic are applied client-side to this universally available dataset, rather than relying on security rules to filter data.

**Access Modeling:** The application's requirements indicate a purely display-oriented interface without user-specific data or collaborative features. Therefore, private data, hierarchical user-owned data, or collaborative data models are not applicable. The public nature of the data aligns with the simplest access model: universal read.

**Data Clarity and Predictability:** The schema for `WeeklyStockGainer` is well-defined, and the collection name `weekly_stock_gainers` is semantically clear, ensuring predictable data access and a clean database structure.
